title: Work-unit manifest + credit-sized term-range chunker with per-chunk CPU/memory caps
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The chunker turns a precision target into independent, credit-sized work units with hard per-chunk CPU/memory caps so each fits one donated session and runaway resource use is prevented. It depends on core-002 and ledger-005.
objective: Implement the credit-sized term-range chunker and the work-unit manifest with hard per-chunk CPU-time and memory caps.
acceptanceCriteria: Splits [0, N) into independent term-range work units, each with a hard CPU-time + memory cap sized to one donated session ('credit-sized'); a unit exceeding its cap aborts cleanly.
Emits a work-unit manifest (id, method, range, caps, algoVersion, backend) that the coordinator and ledger consume.
output: A PR adding the term-range chunker and work-unit manifest with per-chunk CPU/memory caps.
